revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-Hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Suijin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }}
'''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.<ref>Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref>Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1)</ref> == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] gives names her children as Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.<ref>Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref><ref name=":0">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref><ref name=":1">Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co.</ref> where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Suijin before her ascension as Empress Consort. == References ==

revision.comment: Camillz moved page [[Mimaki-hime]] to [[Draft:Mimaki-hime(2)]]: Working on page
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-Hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Suijin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }}
'''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.<ref>Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref>Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1)</ref> == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] gives names her children as Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.<ref>Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref><ref name=":0">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref><ref name=":1">Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co.</ref> where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Suijin before her ascension as Empress Consort. == References ==

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japan]] {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mimaki-Hime}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]] {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Category:kk]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]]

W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mimaki-hime]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Japan]] {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命}} | succession1 = [[Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|qid=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|qid=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|qid=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|qid=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|qid=Q11546050}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11071228]] Wikidata name: Empress Mimakihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:29 BC deaths]] }} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|WD=Q11546050}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11071228]] Wikidata name: Empress Mimakihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:29 BC deaths]] }} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}}

[[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|WD=Q11546050}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11071228]] Wikidata name: Empress Mimakihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | issue = [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.text:
[[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|WD=Q11546050}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11071228]] Wikidata name: Empress Mimakihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | issue = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|WD=Q11546050}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11071228]] Wikidata name: Empress Mimakihime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | issue = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083|1=Yamatohiko|2=ja|3=倭彦命|lt=Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|WD=Q11546050}}}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | issue = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | issue = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[File:大彦命 Ōhiko no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|alt=Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.|left|thumb|455x455px|Mimaki-Hime's father, Prince Ohiko.]] == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | before=[[Saho-hime ]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=97–29 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Ikagashikome]] | after=[[Yasakairi-hime]] | years=appointed in 29 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} [[en:Mimaki-hime]] [[fa:میماکی-هیمه]] [[ja:御間城姫]] [[ko:미마키히메]] [[zh:御间城姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11071228}} [[Category:29 BC deaths]] [[Category:97 BC births]] [[Category:Japan]] [[Category:Royalty]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] [[en:Mimaki-hime]][[fa:میماکی-هیمه]][[ja:御間城姫]][[ko:미마키히메]][[zh:御间城姬]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}|children={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|qid=Q11387083|1=Yamatohiko|2=ja|3=倭彦命|lt=Prince Yamatohiko|qid=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|qid=Q10904824|1=Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|qid=Q10926851|1=Princess Kunikatahime}}; {{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Takenunakawawake|qid=Q11546050|1=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto}}}} {{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan (97 BC–29 BC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Mimaki-Hime
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|御間城姫}}}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 97–29 BC (68 years) | predecessor = [[Ikagashikome]] | successor = [[Saho-hime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} | issue = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} [[Izanomawaka]] [[Kunikatahime]] [[Chijitsukuyamatohime]] [[Igahime]] [[Yamatohiko]] | father = {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|lt=Prince Ohiko|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ohiko|qq=}} | succession1 = [[:en:Empress Dowager of Japan|Empress Dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = 29 BC-? | predecessor1 = Ikagashikome | successor1 = [[Yasakairi-hime]] }} '''Mimaki-Hime''' (御間城姫) was Empress Consort of Japan from 97 BC to 29 BC, and then Empress Dowager from 29 BC to an unknown date.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 165 (Vol. 1) == Life == The [[Nihon Shoki]] tells us her children are Emperor Suinin, Izanomawaka, Kunikatahime, Chijitsukuyamatohime, and Yamatohiko.Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing.Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.Chamberlain, B. H. (1932) “Translation of the Kojiki.” Kobe: J.L. Thompson & Co. where as in the [[Kojiki]] she has another child named Igahime. The Nihon shoki also states that she had her children with Emperor Sujin before her ascension as Empress Consort.{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2024-03-10 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}} While the Nihon Shoki does not give a name for her father, the Kojiki gives her father's name as Prince Ohiko.Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.
